sections.question: Would you please give us a written opinion as to what restrictions, if any, are imposed by the Fair Political Practices Act or your regulations upon the acceptance by the Executive Secretary of the California Public Broadcasting Commission of gifts from members of that Commission?
sections.conclusion: Neither the Political Reform Act nor the regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission impose any restrictions upon acceptance of gifts by the Executive Secretary of the Public Broadcasting Commission from a member of that Commission. However, although the Executive Secretary is not prohibited under the Act or regulations from accepting gifts from a member of the Commission, he may be required to disclose the fact that he received them under the disclosure requirements of the Conflict of Interest Code of the Public Broadcasting Commission.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act or FPPC regulations restrict gifts to the Executive Secretary of the Public Broadcasting Commission from Commission members.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No restrictions exist under the Political Reform Act or FPPC regulations on gift acceptance by the Executive Secretary from Commission members. However, gifts of $25 or more from sources specified in the Public Broadcasting Commission's Conflict of Interest Code must be disclosed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (December 28, 1977) and FPPC response (January 26, 1978). Some text is degraded due to scanning quality, but main sections are legible. Conclusion section was partially reformatted to clarify fragmented text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would you please give us a written opinion as to what restrictions, if any, are imposed by the Fair Political Practices Act or your regulations upon the acceptance by the Executive Secretary of the California Public Broadcasting Commission of gifts from members of that Commission?

CONCLUSION: Neither the Political Reform Act nor the regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission impose any restrictions upon acceptance of gifts by the Executive Secretary of the Public Broadcasting Commission from a member of that Commission. However, although the Executive Secretary is not prohibited under the Act or regulations from accepting gifts from a member of the Commission, he may be required to disclose the fact that he received them under the disclosure requirements of the Conflict of Interest Code of the Public Broadcasting Commission.

Your prompt reply to this request would be appreciated. Sincerely'; / Ed Stokes Director ES:pf
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that the Political Reform Act does not restrict gifts to the Public Broadcasting Commission's Executive Secretary from Commission members, but such gifts may require disclosure under the Commission's Conflict of Interest Code if they meet specified criteria.
